target: SEC-8b.3 L3a threshold-codification paragraph — tighten globe-parasitism closing sentence per curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 Hole 2 (moderate) recommendation. Addresses ISS-1343.
scope_summary: One single-sentence prose patch inside data/sections.json part8b.html — the closing sentence of the L3a (PRED-095) threshold-codification paragraph in SEC-8b.3. Curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 Hole 2 (moderate) flagged that the existing closing sentence ("it is globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission") rests load-bearingly on OPEN-001, but OPEN-001 is narrower than that framing implies — it admits only INPUT-layer globe dependence (no dome-native coordinates), not full physical-calibration dependence. The stronger globe-fingerprint (INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element spherical-Earth coverage geometry) comes from WIN-025, which is independent of OPEN-001. The fix separates the two distinct dependencies — input format (OPEN-001) vs physical calibration (WIN-025) — so the claim no longer hangs on OPEN-001 alone. No verdict change; this is methodological tightening to preempt a dome-defender rebuttal that "OPEN-001 is an engineering TODO about input format, not a physical dependency admission." The non-discrimination thesis is unchanged.
kernel_of_truth: The dome author IS right that PRED-095 has a derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF = V12 H(r)/r). The H(r)/r function IS a dome-geometric formula authored by the dome author. The original sentence overreaches in two directions: (1) "globe-parasitic by construction" suggests the dome's mathematical construction itself is parasitic on globe geometry, but the H(r) function is mathematically self-contained — it could in principle be evaluated at dome-polar r values without ever touching WGS84; (2) "by the dome author's own admission" attaches the strong claim to OPEN-001, but OPEN-001 only admits the current input-format dependency, not a physical-construction dependency. The fix preserves the real critique — every numerical factor that enters PRED-095's evaluation in the current dome implementation traces to globe infrastructure — while attributing that dependency correctly: OPEN-001 covers the input layer (coordinates), WIN-025 covers the physical-calibration layer (INTERMAGNET baseline values, Besselian-element coverage), and both together establish full dependency. Acknowledging the distinction makes the critique more defensible against a dome-defender who would otherwise legitimately complain that we overcounted what OPEN-001 says.

patches.target_locator: part8b.html — SEC-8b.3 L3a threshold-codification paragraph, closing sentence (the "So PRED-095 has a derivation" sentence). HTML-encoded apostrophe (’) — verified single occurrence.
patches.current_text: So PRED-095 has a derivation; it is globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author’s own admission.
patches.proposed_text: So PRED-095 has a derivation; it is globe-dependent at the input layer (per the dome’s own OPEN-001 admission that dome-native coordinates without WGS84 is still IN PROGRESS) and at the physical-calibration layer (per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint: INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element spherical-Earth coverage geometry).
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 Hole 2 (moderate) — the original phrasing reads as if OPEN-001 alone is sufficient to establish "globe-parasitic by construction." OPEN-001 covers the INPUT layer (station-coordinate format) only; the stronger and physically-binding dependency is the calibration layer (INTERMAGNET baselines and Besselian-element coverage), which comes from WIN-025 independently of OPEN-001. The new sentence preserves the substantive critique (PRED-095 has a derivation but is globe-dependent), but attributes the two distinct dependencies to their two distinct sources. "globe-dependent" replaces "globe-parasitic by construction" because the (d) closing-paragraph sentence retains "globe-parasitic by construction" framing keyed to BOTH WIN-025 AND OPEN-001 — and the (d) framing is already defensible because it cites both pillars. The L3a sentence is the overreach version; the (d) sentence is the keyed-to-both summary, so they harmonize when L3a softens to "globe-dependent at the input layer ... and at the physical-calibration layer." The italicized "and" emphasizes the conjunction so the reader sees both dependencies as separate strands rather than restatements of one.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The argumentative thrust is unchanged: PRED-095 has a derivation; every numerical input traces to globe infrastructure; the dome cannot currently evaluate its own predictions without globe coordinates AND globe-measured baselines. Only the rhetorical framing tightens from "by the dome author's own admission" (which loaded OPEN-001 with more weight than it carries) to a two-strand attribution (OPEN-001 = input format, WIN-025 = physical calibration). The link to WIN-025 already exists in the same paragraph ("per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint, BOU and W004 baselines are INTERMAGNET globe-measured observations"); the new closing sentence completes the L3a paragraph by returning to that link explicitly. No change to verdict, no change to numerical claims, no change to the L3a layer's role in the four-layer audit stack.

anticipated_objections.objection: The original phrase "globe-parasitic by construction" was a stronger rhetorical move and helped the section punch. Softening to "globe-dependent" weakens the critique.
anticipated_objections.response: The (d) closing paragraph retains "globe-parasitic by construction" — keyed to BOTH WIN-025 and OPEN-001 as the source citations — so the strong framing is preserved at the section summary. The L3a paragraph is the per-layer detail where the audit-grade attribution matters; there the wording should match what the sources actually support. The (d) summary is allowed to compress because it cites both pillars; the L3a detail is not allowed to compress in the same way because it stands alone at the threshold-codification claim. The two-tier structure (detail attributes carefully, summary compresses) is intentional. Softening the per-layer attribution is not a retreat from the section-level claim; it is hygiene that strengthens the section against a defender who would otherwise note the OPEN-001 overreach.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just keep the original sentence and add a footnote citing WIN-025 alongside OPEN-001? That preserves the rhetorical force AND closes the attribution gap.
anticipated_objections.response: A footnote layered onto an overreach sentence does not undo the overreach in the main text. A reader who skims will read the main sentence — "globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission" — and absorb the OPEN-001-alone framing without registering the footnote. The fix needs to be visible at the sentence the reader actually reads. Additionally, OPEN-001 specifically admits only the coordinate-format dependency; appending WIN-025 as a parenthetical to that sentence would still leave the "by the dome author's own admission" clause inviting the rebuttal "my admission is narrower than you're framing it." Replacing the sentence is the cleaner fix.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new sentence is longer and breaks the paragraph's rhythm. The original was a punchy closer; this one is two clauses joined by italicized "and."
anticipated_objections.response: The original L3a paragraph is already four long sentences of audit-grade detail; the closing sentence is meant to compress the paragraph's case, not to deliver a punchline. The paragraph's rhetorical climax is the body content (FSF = V12 H(r)/r; baselines are INTERMAGNET; coverage is Besselian-element; OPEN-001 admits coordinate dependency), not the closing one-liner. The new sentence preserves the closing function ("so PRED-095 has a derivation [but]...") while making the [but] clause attribution-accurate. Length increases by ~30 words but information density also increases — the reader gets two distinct strands of dependency cited to two distinct sources rather than one rolled-together claim.

tldr_review: The 8b.3 TLDR reads: "Adopted as steel-man: we restructure our eclipse content into the four-layer presentation and publish a codified Tier 3 magnetic threshold. Two structural critiques survive adoption — an ensemble 'anchor' fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor; and the magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe, because the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo fit on the same body of past eclipse-magnetic observations." The TLDR makes NO claim about globe-parasitism — it focuses on the structural arithmetic-sharing critique. No TLDR update needed; the patched sentence is interior to the L3a detail and does not affect the section-level TLDR statement.
test_impact: None expected. data/sections.json string-content edit on part8b.html field, one single-sentence replacement. tests/run.sh should pass without modification — there are no numerical claims, layer counts, rates, or classifier outputs touched.
integration_instructions: 1) Apply PATCH-400-A to data/sections.json part8b.html (find_pattern / replace_pattern style on the verbatim current_text). 2) Mark ISS-1343 status=fixed-pending-verification with fix_details referencing EXP-400. 3) Rebuild HTML (node build.js html) and run tests/run.sh.
notes_for_curmudgeon: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 Hole 2 (moderate) recommended this exact rewording ("globe-dependent at the input layer (per OPEN-001) AND at the physical-calibration layer (per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint: INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element spherical-Earth coverage)"). EXP-400 implements that recommendation with three small refinements: (1) expanded OPEN-001 citation to inline what OPEN-001 says ("dome-native coordinates without WGS84 is still IN PROGRESS") so the reader sees the narrow scope of the admission rather than taking "per OPEN-001" on faith; (2) italicized "and" between the two layers for visual emphasis; (3) preserved the existing #win025 inline link with its showTab() handler so the cross-reference remains clickable. ISS-1344 (sibling moderate in same review, c4 Hole 3 — inject_ai_layer.py family-relationship reframing) is NOT addressed in this EXP; it requires a separate patch on the "L3a non-discrimination & internal heterogeneity" paragraph and is deferred to the next analyst run. summary_for_decider: ISS-1344 should remain assigned-analyst until the next Mode 1 cycle picks it up. No override of class_hint (was null) — this is verification-class work per the recommendation's narrow text-replacement scope.
summary_for_decider: Single-sentence patch on SEC-8b.3 L3a closing sentence per curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 Hole 2 (moderate). review_class=verification because the work is narrow text-replacement of a curmudgeon-specified rewording; no new arguments, no verdict change, no cross-section impact. The (d) closing-paragraph sentence retains "globe-parasitic by construction" framing keyed to both WIN-025 and OPEN-001 — that framing is already defensible and is NOT patched here. Sibling ISS-1344 (c4 Hole 3, inject_ai_layer.py family-relationship reframing) remains pending for next analyst run; this EXP addresses ISS-1343 only.
